# WI-QUIC-CRT-0028 - QUIC CRT detached resumption-ticket carrier handoff work item

## Summary

Export an opaque detached resumption-ticket carrier from the managed client/runtime path and store it as dormant internal state in a later client setup without widening the supported promise.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0130

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0028

## Planned Changes

- A new internal immutable detached ticket carrier is exported from the runtime-owned snapshot after real ticket ingress.
- The carrier threads through the later managed client setup path and is stored as separate dormant internal state.
- The dormant state remains distinct from transient bridge-state storage and the current-lifetime runtime-owned snapshot.
- Focused requirement-home tests cover export, absence before capture, dormant import, duplicate-update non-replacement, server-role rejection, explicit early-data closure before and after handoff, unchanged handshake/bootstrap behavior, and the public no-hand-off/no-resumption/no-early-data guard.
- PSK derivation, 0-RTT packet processing, ClientHello replay, replay ownership, transfer, retry, key update, and any public API widening remain out of scope.

## Out of Scope

- public API widening
- ticket replay into ClientHello
- PSK derivation
- resumed handshake support
- 0-RTT packet send/receive support
- anti-replay behavior
- broad support for arbitrary post-handshake TLS messages
- fake replay or fake resumption success paths
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- key update
- broader session-management infrastructure
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to detached handoff or dormant import

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for detached carrier export, absence before capture, dormant import into later client setup, duplicate-update non-replacement, server-role rejection, explicit early-data closure before and after handoff, the public no-hand-off/no-resumption/no-early-data guard, and unchanged handshake/bootstrap behavior with dormant state present; run the current CRT/API/INT guards and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep; run repo-local SpecTrace validation and render checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Completion Notes

This is a landed narrow cross-lifetime handoff slice. It exports an opaque detached resumption-ticket carrier from the managed client/runtime path and stores it as dormant internal state in a later client setup; it does not claim PSK derivation, replay handling, 0-RTT support, or any public ticket/early-data promise.
